query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
(540011, 'What is the primary purpose of identifying the main idea in a text?', '["To find all the details","To understand the central message","To count the paragraphs","To identify the author"]', 1, 1),
(540011, 'Which of the following best describes supporting details?', '["The title of the text","Information that backs up the main idea","The conclusion of the text","The author\'s biography"]', 1, 2),
(540011, 'How can you identify an author\'s purpose?', '["By reading the first sentence","By looking at the publication date","By analyzing what the author is trying to communicate","By counting the number of paragraphs"]', 2, 3),
(540011, 'What does \'tone\' refer to in writing?', '["The volume of the voice","The author\'s attitude toward the subject","The length of the text","The number of characters"]', 1, 4),
(540011, 'Which is an example of bias in writing?', '["Using facts and evidence","Presenting only one side of an issue","Using clear language","Using proper grammar"]', 1, 5),
(540011, 'What is an inference?', '["A direct statement in the text","A conclusion based on evidence and reasoning","The title of the passage","A list of facts"]', 1, 6),
(540011, 'How do supporting details strengthen a text?', '["They confuse the reader","They provide evidence for the main idea","They change the topic","They make the text longer"]', 1, 7),
(540011, 'What is the difference between fact and opinion?', '["Facts are longer","Facts can be proven; opinions are beliefs","Opinions are always correct","Facts are in the first paragraph"]', 1, 8),
(540011, 'Which reading strategy helps you understand unfamiliar words?', '["Skipping them","Using context clues","Guessing randomly","Ignoring them"]', 1, 9),
(540011, 'What does \'summarizing\' mean?', '["Reading the entire text twice","Stating the main ideas briefly","Writing a new story","Changing the author\'s words"]', 1, 10),
(540011, 'How can you determine if a source is credible?', '["By its length","By checking the author\'s expertise and evidence","By its popularity","By its color scheme"]', 1, 11),
(540012, 'What are context clues?', '["Words in bold","Information surrounding an unfamiliar word","The title of the text","The author\'s name"]', 1, 1),
(540012, 'Which context clue type provides a definition?', '["Contrast clue","Definition clue","Example clue","Comparison clue"]', 1, 2),
(540012, 'What is a prefix?', '["A word at the end","A word at the beginning that changes meaning","A type of sentence","A punctuation mark"]', 1, 3),
(540012, 'What does the prefix \'un-\' mean?', '["Again","Not","Before","After"]', 1, 4),
(540012, 'What is a suffix?', '["A word at the beginning","A word at the end that changes meaning","A type of paragraph","A synonym"]', 1, 5),
(540012, 'What does the suffix \'-tion\' typically indicate?', '["An adjective","A noun (often an action)","A verb","An adverb"]', 1, 6),
(540012, 'What is a root word?', '["A word with no meaning","The base word to which prefixes/suffixes are added","A type of plant","A synonym"]', 1, 7),
(540012, 'In the sentence \'The benevolent teacher helped all students,\' what does \'benevolent\' mean?', '["Strict","Kind and generous","Tired","Angry"]', 1, 8),
(540012, 'What is a synonym?', '["A word that means the opposite","A word that means the same or nearly the same","A word with no meaning","A type of prefix"]', 1, 9),
(540012, 'What is an antonym?', '["A word that means the same","A word that means the opposite","A type of suffix","A root word"]', 1, 10),
(540012, 'How can understanding word families help with vocabulary?', '["It confuses readers","It helps you understand related words","It makes reading slower","It has no effect"]', 1, 11),
(540013, 'What is a sentence?', '["A group of random words","A complete thought with a subject and predicate","A paragraph","A list of words"]', 1, 1),
(540013, 'What is the subject of a sentence?', '["The action","The person or thing doing the action","The object","The verb"]', 1, 2),
(540013, 'What is the predicate of a sentence?', '["The subject","The verb and what it does","The object","The adjective"]', 1, 3),
(540013, 'What are the parts of speech?', '["Sentences and paragraphs","Nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, etc.","Only verbs and nouns","Only adjectives"]', 1, 4),
(540013, 'What is a noun?', '["An action word","A person, place, or thing","A describing word","A connecting word"]', 1, 5),
(540013, 'What is a verb?', '["A person or thing","An action or state of being","A describing word","A connecting word"]', 1, 6),
(540013, 'What is an adjective?', '["An action word","A person or thing","A word that describes a noun","A connecting word"]', 1, 7),
(540013, 'What is an adverb?', '["A person or thing","A describing word for nouns","A word that describes a verb, adjective, or adverb","A connecting word"]', 1, 8),
(540013, 'What is a simple sentence?', '["A short sentence","A sentence with one independent clause","A sentence with many words","A sentence without verbs"]', 1, 9),
(540013, 'What is a compound sentence?', '["A sentence with adjectives","Two independent clauses joined together","A sentence with many nouns","A very long sentence"]', 1, 10),
(540013, 'What is a complex sentence?', '["A difficult sentence","An independent clause with one or more dependent clauses","A sentence with many words","A sentence without punctuation"]', 1, 11),
(540014, 'When should you use a comma?', '["Never","To separate items in a list or clauses","Only at the end of sentences","Randomly"]', 1, 1),
(540014, 'What is the purpose of a semicolon?', '["To end a sentence","To connect two independent clauses","To separate words","To indicate a question"]', 1, 2),
(540014, 'When do you use a colon?', '["To end a sentence","To introduce a list or explanation","To separate words","To indicate a question"]', 1, 3),
(540014, 'What is the purpose of an apostrophe?', '["To end a sentence","To show possession or contractions","To separate items","To indicate a question"]', 1, 4),
(540014, 'How do you form a possessive noun?', '["Add \'ing\'","Add an apostrophe and \'s\'","Add \'ed\'","Add \'ly\'"]', 1, 5),
(540014, 'What is a contraction?', '["A long word","A shortened form of two words combined","A type of sentence","A punctuation mark"]', 1, 6),
(540014, 'When should you capitalize a word?', '["Never","Always","At the beginning of sentences and for proper nouns","Only in titles"]', 2, 7),
(540014, 'What is a proper noun?', '["A common noun","A specific person, place, or thing that is capitalized","A verb","An adjective"]', 1, 8),
(540014, 'When do you use a question mark?', '["At the end of statements","At the end of questions","In the middle of sentences","Never"]', 1, 9),
(540014, 'When do you use an exclamation mark?', '["For questions","For statements","To show strong emotion or emphasis","Never"]', 2, 10),
(540014, 'What is the purpose of quotation marks?', '["To end sentences","To indicate direct speech or quoted material","To separate paragraphs","To show possession"]', 1, 11),
(540015, 'What is the first step in the writing process?', '["Editing","Prewriting/Planning","Publishing","Revising"]', 1, 1),
(540015, 'What happens during the prewriting stage?', '["Correcting errors","Generating ideas and planning","Publishing the work","Reading others\' work"]', 1, 2),
(540015, 'What is the purpose of drafting?', '["To correct errors","To create the first version of writing","To publish the work","To plan the writing"]', 1, 3),
(540015, 'What is revising?', '["Changing the main ideas","Making major changes to improve content and organization","Publishing the work","Planning the writing"]', 1, 4),
(540015, 'What is editing?', '["Changing the main ideas","Correcting grammar, spelling, and punctuation","Publishing the work","Planning the writing"]', 1, 5),
(540015, 'What is the purpose of peer review?', '["To grade the work","To get feedback from others","To publish the work","To plan the writing"]', 1, 6),
(540015, 'What is a topic sentence?', '["The last sentence in a paragraph","The sentence that states the main idea of a paragraph","The first word in a paragraph","A question"]', 1, 7),
(540015, 'What should a paragraph contain?', '["Only one sentence","A topic sentence and supporting details","Only adjectives","No verbs"]', 1, 8),
(540015, 'What is a thesis statement?', '["A question","The main idea of an entire essay","A conclusion","An introduction"]', 1, 9),
(540015, 'How should ideas be organized in a paragraph?', '["Randomly","Logically with clear connections","Backwards","By length"]', 1, 10),
(540015, 'What is a transition word?', '["A type of noun","A word that connects ideas","A verb","An adjective"]', 1, 11),
(540016, 'What is an argument?', '["A disagreement","A position supported by evidence and reasoning","A story","A question"]', 1, 1),
(540016, 'What is a claim?', '["A question","A statement of position or belief","A fact","An opinion"]', 1, 2),
(540016, 'What is evidence?', '["An opinion","Information that supports a claim","A story","A question"]', 1, 3),
(540016, 'What types of evidence can support an argument?', '["Only opinions","Facts, statistics, examples, and expert opinions","Only stories","Only questions"]', 1, 4),
(540016, 'What is a counterargument?', '["An agreement","An opposing viewpoint","A conclusion","An introduction"]', 1, 5),
(540016, 'Why should you address counterarguments?', '["To agree with opponents","To strengthen your argument by showing you considered other views","To confuse readers","To change your position"]', 1, 6),
(540016, 'What is logical reasoning?', '["Making random statements","Using facts and evidence to support conclusions","Telling stories","Asking questions"]', 1, 7),
(540016, 'What is a logical fallacy?', '["A correct argument","A flawed reasoning pattern","A type of evidence","A conclusion"]', 1, 8),
(540016, 'What should a strong conclusion do?', '["Introduce new ideas","Restate the main claim and summarize key points","Ask questions","Tell a story"]', 1, 9),
(540016, 'How should you organize an argumentative essay?', '["Randomly","Introduction, body paragraphs with evidence, conclusion","Conclusion first","No organization needed"]', 1, 10),
(540016, 'What is persuasive language?', '["Neutral language","Language designed to convince or influence","Technical language","Poetic language"]', 1, 11),
(540017, 'What is a theme?', '["A character\'s name","A central idea or message in a work","A setting","A plot twist"]', 1, 1),
(540017, 'What is characterization?', '["The setting of a story","The way an author develops and reveals character traits","The main plot","The ending"]', 1, 2),
(540017, 'What is a protagonist?', '["The villain","The main character","A minor character","The narrator"]', 1, 3),
(540017, 'What is an antagonist?', '["The main character","The force opposing the protagonist","A supporting character","The narrator"]', 1, 4),
(540017, 'What is plot?', '["The setting","The sequence of events in a story","The theme","The characters"]', 1, 5),
(540017, 'What are the main parts of plot structure?', '["Only beginning and end","Exposition, rising action, climax, falling action, resolution","Only middle","No structure"]', 1, 6),
(540017, 'What is symbolism?', '["Literal meaning","Using objects or actions to represent ideas","A type of character","A plot device"]', 1, 7),
(540017, 'What is a literary device?', '["A book","A technique used to create meaning or effect","A character","A setting"]', 1, 8),
(540017, 'What is foreshadowing?', '["Explaining the ending","Hinting at future events","Describing the past","Introducing characters"]', 1, 9),
(540017, 'What is irony?', '["A type of character","When something is opposite to what is expected","A plot twist","A setting"]', 1, 10),
(540017, 'What is the point of view in a story?', '["The author\'s opinion","The perspective from which the story is told","The setting","The theme"]', 1, 11),
(540018, 'What is informational text?', '["A story","Text designed to inform or explain","Poetry","A play"]', 1, 1),
(540018, 'What is the purpose of a non-fiction text?', '["To entertain only","To provide factual information","To confuse readers","To tell a story"]', 1, 2),
(540018, 'How can you identify key information in a text?', '["By reading randomly","By looking for main ideas and supporting details","By skipping paragraphs","By ignoring headings"]', 1, 3),
(540018, 'What is source credibility?', '["How long the text is","The reliability and authority of the source","The number of words","The font size"]', 1, 4),
(540018, 'How can you evaluate a source\'s credibility?', '["By its popularity","By checking author expertise, evidence, and bias","By its length","By its appearance"]', 1, 5),
(540018, 'What is bias in informational text?', '["Presenting facts","Favoring one perspective over others","Using clear language","Using evidence"]', 1, 6),
(540018, 'What is the difference between fact and opinion in non-fiction?', '["They are the same","Facts are provable; opinions are beliefs","Opinions are always correct","Facts are shorter"]', 1, 7),
(540018, 'What organizational pattern uses cause and effect?', '["Chronological","Showing why something happens and its results","Comparison","Problem-solution"]', 1, 8),
(540018, 'What is a primary source?', '["A book about history","An original document or firsthand account","A summary","A textbook"]', 1, 9),
(540018, 'What is a secondary source?', '["An original document","A source that interprets or analyzes primary sources","A firsthand account","An interview"]', 1, 10),
(540018, 'Why is it important to evaluate multiple sources?', '["To waste time","To get different perspectives and verify information","To confuse yourself","It\'s not important"]', 1, 11);
